items.title: Penang pumps RM129,900 into youth ideas, not just events
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 21 — The Penang government has approved RM129,900 under its Youth Development Fund this year to support 68 youth development programmes by 48 associations in the state. Penang Youth, Sports and Health Committee chairman Daniel Gooi Zi Sen said the funds were part of a total of RM200,000 allocated by the state government under the Youth State Executive Council Meeting. “The funds are a strategic development to organise 68 programmes encompassing aspects such as skills, marketability, volunteerism and leadership.

items.title: Malaysia's hydrography role crucial in keeping Strait Of Malacca safe and open
items.summary: PORT KLANG: Malaysia's hydrography capabilities play a critical role in ensuring the Strait of Malacca remains safe and open, says National Hydrography Centre. Director-general Rear Admiral Dr Najhan Md Said said the strait was a vital artery for the global economy, handling traffic volumes more than three times that of the Strait of Hormuz. He said safeguarding the strait could not rest on a single agency, but required coordinated efforts among all maritime safety stakeholders.

items.title: Tekun targets 24-hour loan approvals for small businesses
items.summary: MELAKA, June 21 — Tabung Ekonomi Kumpulan Usaha Niaga (Tekun Nasional) aims to approve financing below RM20,000 within 24 hours, said Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong. Sim said the quick-approval target helps Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) secure working capital immediately. Meanwhile, the ministry said in a statement that Tekun Nasional financing applications up to RM100,000 are processed and approved within seven days.

items.title: Anwar pays condolence visit to Nancy Shukri
items.summary: KUCHING: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid a condolence visit to Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri following the passing of her husband, Datuk Kamil Misuari. Anwar arrived at Nancy's residence at No. Accompanied by Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas, he spent about 20 minutes at the residence before leaving for the Borneo Convention Centre Kuching to attend another function.

items.title: Bintulu Port shift marks win-win under MA63 framework, says federal minister
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — The handover of Bintulu Port’s regulatory authority to the Sarawak state government is among the key achievements in the implementation of the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63), said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud. “Bintulu’s strategic location, coupled with Sarawak’s advantage in renewable energy resources, particularly hydroelectric power, makes it increasingly attractive to investors shifting towards low-carbon operations. The statement was issued following a cheque handover ceremony marking the change in status of Bintulu Port from a federal port to a state port, which took place in Bintulu today.

items.title: Zambry: Malaysia emerging as global education hub after strong Asia rankings showing
items.summary: Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said that while rankings are not the ultimate goal of a university, they remain an important indicator of the quality, reputation and competitiveness of higher education institutions on the international stage. He also congratulated Universiti Teknologi Petronas (UTP) for becoming the first Malaysian university to secure a place among Asia’s top 40 universities. These institutions include Universiti Malaya (UM), Sunway University, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM), Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM), Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM), Universiti Utara Malaysia (UUM), Universiti Malaysia Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah (UMPSA), Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) and Universiti Tenaga Nasional (UNITEN).

items.title: Al Jazeera rejects Israeli accusations against journalist killed in Gaza
items.summary: DOHA: Al Jazeera today rejected Israeli accusations that one of its journalists, killed in Gaza a day earlier, was a Hamas operative, as family and colleagues mourned the cameraman in the Palestinian territory. The Qatar-based network said in a statement that it "condemns the Israeli occupation army's baseless accusations, which seek to justify its crimes against Al Jazeera journalists and cameramen in Gaza, most recently the killing of cameraman Ahmed Wishah." "Alongside his work as an Al Jazeera photojournalist in recent years, Wishah was an operative in Hamas' military wing," the military said.

items.title: Woman, accomplice remanded over alleged staged kidnapping for ransom
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two individuals, including a woman believed to have staged her own kidnapping to obtain RM18,000 in ransom money, have been remanded for 14 days beginning today to assist in investigations. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the remand order was obtained from the Johor Bahru Magistrate's Court today. However, Kumar said preliminary investigations revealed inconsistencies in the alleged victim's account of the kidnapping, leading to her arrest to assist in investigations.

items.title: Traveller scheme for Malaysia-Singapore workers in final stage, says Ramanan
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The proposal paper for the Traveller scheme, which provides social protection for Malaysians working abroad, particularly in Singapore, is in its final stages ahead of implementation. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the scheme, expected to benefit about 480,000 Malaysians commuting daily to Singapore, would be tabled in the upcoming parliamentary session starting tomorrow. He said the ministry and the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) had completed the proposal paper and were finalising approvals, expected by August before enforcement.

items.title: Fahmi calls for greater recognition, support for local music industry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The local music industry should be recognised and supported for its important contribution to bringing people together and helping shape Malaysia's cultural identity, said Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Fahmi also paid tribute to those in the creative industry for their enduring contributions to advancing the nation's music industry. He also expressed hope that the local music industry would continue to flourish and receive the appreciation it deserves.

items.title: From Gus Fring to Shahada: Breaking Bad star Giancarlo Esposito embraces Islam
items.summary: ANKARA: American actor Giancarlo Esposito, best known for his role as Gus Fring in the television series Breaking Bad, has embraced Islam during a stay in Saudi Arabia, Anadolu Ajansi reported, citing local media. Saudi Gazette reported that General Entertainment Authority chairman Turki Al-Sheikh said Esposito recited the Shahada, the Islamic declaration of faith, and joined members of a production team in prayer at a mosque while filming in the kingdom. He said Esposito also expressed appreciation for the community and hospitality he encountered during filming.

items.title: Sabah ramps up enforcement of amended fisheries act
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah Fisheries Department will strengthen manpower, technology and cooperation to ensure effective enforcement of the amended fisheries law in state waters. Its director Datin Dr Shuhadah Mustapha said enforcement capacity would be improved through training, monitoring systems such as the Integrated Vessel Monitoring System, and cooperation with national security agencies. She said enforcement would involve joint operations between the Fisheries Department, the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency, police and navy, with emphasis on monitoring the Exclusive Economic Zone and protecting sensitive marine areas.

items.title: Older Persons Bill crucial amid ageing nation challenges, says Lee Lam Thye
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The proposed Older Persons Bill is timely and essential, as Malaysia grapples with the challenges of an ageing population, said Senior Citizens Advisory Council member Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye. He said the number of senior citizens in the country is rising rapidly, alongside longer life expectancy. "Many senior citizens today face various challenges, including neglect, financial problems, loneliness, poor access to healthcare, abuse and social isolation.

items.title: Viral data leak claims old incident, unrelated to current platforms - NSC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The National Security Council (NSC) has clarified that claims of a personal data leak circulating on social media refer to information believed to have originated from cybersecurity incidents reported before 2022 and are not linked to any current platform. The council through the National Cyber Security Agency (NACSA), said the information was believed to have been unlawfully obtained through cyber intrusions targeting various systems prior to 2022 and is now being redistributed through online platforms without authorisation. "NACSA emphasises that providing, disseminating or granting access to information obtained unlawfully constitutes an offence under Malaysian law, even if the service is hosted outside the country," the council said in a statement said.

items.title: Five remanded after missing teenage cousins found safe in Machang
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Police have arrested five men in connection with the disappearance of two teenage cousins from Kampung Baloh near Kok Lanas, who were found safe in Machang yesterday after being reported missing since Thursday night. Machang police chief Superintendent Azuhar Md Nor said the suspects were detained in Machang yesterday evening. He said police obtained remand orders against all five men at the Machang magistrate's court this morning to facilitate investigations.

items.title: Johor polls: Stop dragging royal institution into politics, says Ramanan
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has criticised political leaders for dragging the royal institution into political manoeuvring ahead of the 16th Johor election. "When we want to fight, we must fight with integrity. "You must fight your own battles.

items.title: Malaysia's automotive sector in high gear despite May setback
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's automotive sector is expected to stay resilient in 2026, with total industry volume (TIV) forecast to ease slightly to 780,000 units as national brands continue to dominate the market. Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said May TIV fell 15.1 per cent month-on-month and 12.1 per cent year-on-year, mainly due to Perodua's temporary plant maintenance shutdown. "Our top picks for the sector remain MBM Resources Bhd and Sime Darby Bhd, given their leverage onto Perodua's sustaining sales volume," HLIB said.

items.title: Viral data‑leak claims stem from pre‑2022 intrusions, unrelated to current systems, says National Security Council
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — The National Security Council (MKN) has clarified that claims of a personal data leak circulating on social media refer to information believed to have originated from cybersecurity incidents reported before 2022 and are not linked to any current platform. The council through the National Cyber Security Agency (NACSA), said the information was believed to have been unlawfully obtained through cyber intrusions targeting various systems prior to 2022 and is now being redistributed through online platforms without authorisation. “NACSA emphasises that providing, disseminating or granting access to information obtained unlawfully constitutes an offence under Malaysian law, even if the service is hosted outside the country,” the council said in a statement said.

items.title: Old bruises, unanswered questions: Death of 64-year-old at Sungai Petani care home sparks police probe
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 21 — A 64-year-old man’s death at a care centre here has sparked a police investigation after his family alleged he had previously been involved in a fight with another resident. Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said the family lodged a report yesterday and an investigation is now underway, Harian Metro reported today. “Police have recorded statements from relevant individuals to assist in the investigation,” he added.

items.title: Africa, Asia increase food imports from Russia amid growing el-Nino concerns
items.summary: MOSCOW: Countries across Africa, parts of Asia, particularly West Asia, are increasing imports of Russian food products amid uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz and concerns over the approaching El Nino climate phenomenon, according to Russia's state-owned Rosselkhozbank (RSHB) on Sunday, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti. El Nino is a characterised by unusually warm sea surface temperatures in the equatorial Pacific Ocean. In particular, amid instability in the Strait of Hormuz and anticipated El Nino-related climate fluctuations, many countries in Africa, parts of Asia, particularly West Asia are building up agricultural stockpiles due to concerns over potential fertiliser shortages and droughts," the bank said.

items.title: A-GC, MACC urged to publish reasons for corruption compounds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) and the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) should publish summaries explaining decisions to compound corruption-related offences, particularly in high-profile cases, says an anti-graft watchdog. Malaysia Corruption Watch (MCW) said the move would help strengthen public confidence in the justice system. "In high-profile cases, the A-GC and MACC should consider publishing a summary of reasons for compound decisions without affecting the confidentiality of investigations," said MCW president Jais Abdul Karim.

items.title: Territorial Army vital in strengthening national security, says Raja Muda of Perlis
items.summary: ARAU: The Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, said the Territorial Army will continue to uphold its role under the concept of "To Raise, Train and Maintain" personnel. "Meanwhile, the concept of 'maintain' ensures their readiness is consistently preserved for augmentation purposes at any time," His Royal Highness said when officiating the closing parade ceremony of the Basic Volunteer Soldier Course for the army. His Highness said Sharifah Farah Adriana's completion of the course clearly demonstrates that national defence is a shared responsibility.

items.title: Pickleball row sparks calls for stricter council control on court approvals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral dispute over a late-night pickleball game in Petaling Jaya has sparked wider debate over whether local councils should play a greater role in approving court locations and setting operating hours near residential areas. Some netizens argued that city councils should take greater responsibility in approving court locations, building specifications and operating hours. "City councils need to play a part in approving court location, the building structure establishment and operating hours," said commenter Willie Jee.

items.title: Job losses near 39,000 in five months with Malaysians aged 25 to 39 hardest hit, says HR Ministry
items.summary: According to data cited by ETHRWorldSEA, the Human Resources Ministry said 38,953 workers were recorded as having lost their jobs between January and May under the Employment Insurance System (EIS). Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said most of those affected were aged between 25 and 39. Ramanan said the ministry is expanding employment and skills programmes to help affected workers secure new jobs, enhance their skills and transition into industries with stronger demand.

items.title: Missing cousins found safe after two-day search, five arrested
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Two teenage cousins who had been missing since Thursday night were found safe in Machang yesterday evening, about 32 hours after their families lodged separate police reports on Friday morning. Nor Syahzanani Mohd Sulaiman, 15, and Nur Kaseh Nalisya Mohd Ramzul Nazrie, 16, were found at a homestay in Machang, about 25km from their homes in Kampung Baloh near Kok Lanas. Police have since arrested five suspects to assist in investigations into the girls' disappearance.

items.title: Police tracking man over Taman Puchong Prima house trespass
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are looking for a man, caught on a closed-circuit television (CCTV), trespassing into a house in Taman Puchong Prima, Subang Jaya. Subang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Wan Azlan Wan Mamat said police received a report on the incident at 3pm yesterday. "Initial investigations and review of the CCTV footage found that an unknown man had trespassed into the back area of the complainant's house.

items.title: Legal action likely over Tambirat Waterfront project collapse, delaying Phase Two and the regatta, says Sarawak minister
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — The stalled Phase One of the Tambirat Waterfront project is likely to end up in court following the collapse of part of the riverside development, a setback that has also delayed the return of the long-awaited Tambirat Regatta, said Datuk Seri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah. The Minister of Youth, Sports and Entrepreneur Development said the regatta had originally been planned to coincide with the completion of the waterfront’s first phase, with Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg expected to attend the event. According to Abdul Karim, the waterfront project, initiated before the last state election, comprises two phases.

items.title: Malaysia’s shrimp supply stable as industry backs temporary Thai import ban, says DOF
items.summary: MARANG, June 21 — Malaysia’s shrimp supply is guaranteed to be sufficient even after the country imposed a temporary ban on five shrimp species from Thailand starting June 1. Department of Fisheries (DOF) director-general, Datuk Adnan Hussain, said shrimp associations and local farmers have drawn up plans to raise production levels to cater for domestic consumption and overseas markets. He said Malaysia records annual shrimp imports of about 11,000 metric tonnes from Thailand and exports roughly 3,000 metric tonnes in return.

items.title: Malaysia well-positioned to lead in energy transition financing: TNB chief new energy officer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Overwhelming response to two Islamic bond issuances by Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) totalling RM2.5 billion for energy transition projects shows that Malaysia is well-positioned to lead in energy transition financing within the region. These two sukuk issuances in 2026 which comprised RM1 billion for solar in May and RM1.5 billion for hydro in March, were both oversubscribed by five times, TNB's chief new energy officer Mohd Zarihi Mohd Hashim said. He said Malaysia's National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR) estimates a financing requirement of RM1.2 trillion to RM1.3 trillion over the next 25 years, or approximately RM50 billion annually.

items.title: Police seal Bercham storm‑hit areas, tighten movement controls to curb theft
items.summary: IPOH, June 21 — Police have sealed off five zones within disaster-affected areas in Bercham and restricted movement in and out to prevent theft or house break-ins. Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said some flexibility is given to residents who wish to enter or leave the affected areas, particularly those cleaning up their homes at night. Among the areas affected are Anjung Bercham Utara, Taman Mujur, Kampung Bercham, Kampung Tersusun Tasek, Taman Pusat Bercham and Taman Indah Sakti.

items.title: Hassan: Federal allocations to Johor governed by Constitution
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Madani Government is guided by the law and the Federal Constitution in matters involving financial allocations to states, including Johor, said Pasir Gudang member of parliament Hassan Abdul Karim. In a statement, the lawyer said Article 109(1) of the Federal Constitution provides that the Federal Government shall, for every financial year, grant each state a capitation allocation in accordance with Part I of the Tenth Schedule. As such, Hassan said any Johor government formed after the Johor election on July 11 could refer issues concerning federal allocations to the National Finance Council in accordance with existing legal provisions.

items.title: Local shrimp supply unaffected by Thai import ban - Fisheries Dept
items.summary: MARANG: Malaysia's shrimp supply is guaranteed to be sufficient even after the country imposed a temporary ban on five shrimp species from Thailand starting June 1. Fisheries Department director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said shrimp associations and local farmers have drawn up plans to raise production levels to cater for domestic consumption and overseas markets. He said Malaysia records annual shrimp imports of about 11,000 metric tonnes from Thailand and exports roughly 3,000 metric tonnes in return.

items.title: MOF: Diesel price to drop to RM2.10 per litre from July 2026 under MyKad‑based subsidy mechanism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — The subsidised diesel price for Malaysians will fall to RM2.10 per litre starting July 2026, following an announcement by Prime Minister and Finance Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim in Bintulu today. The ministry said the new mechanism will rely on MyKad verification to ensure that only Malaysians receive subsidised diesel, stating that “diesel subsidies will be channelled directly to Malaysians through MyKad verification, while non‑citizens and ineligible parties must purchase diesel at the unsubsidised price.” The statement said the reform mirrors the approach used for BUDI Madani RON95 (BUDI95) and will unify both the price and method of subsidy distribution across the country. The ministry said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan will provide further details tomorrow.

items.title: Bersatu reasserts founding role in PN amid Pas row
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has reminded Pas of the history behind the formation of Perikatan Nasional (PN), insisting the coalition originated from an idea by Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin. Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said the origins of PN could not be disputed, as the formation process was led by Bersatu from the outset. He said that Bersatu managed the PN registration process until its official approval.

items.title: Federal allocations to states guided by Constitution, says Pasir Gudang MP
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 21 — The Madani Government is guided by written laws and the Federal Constitution in matters involving financial allocations to states, including Johor, said Pasir Gudang MP Hassan Abdul Karim. In a statement, he said Article 109(1) of the Federal Constitution provides that the Federal Government shall, for every financial year, grant each state a capitation allocation in accordance with the provisions of Part I of the Tenth Schedule. As such, he said any Johor state government formed after the Johor state election on July 11 may refer issues concerning Federal Government allocations to the National Finance Council in accordance with existing legal provisions.

items.title: [UPDATED] Govt lowers subsidised diesel price to RM2.10 from next month
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will lower the price of subsidised diesel for Malaysians to RM2.10 per litre from July as part of a nationwide subsidy rationalisation programme. The ministry said the reform would ensure Malaysians enjoy the same price and purchasing method nationwide, with diesel subsidies channelled directly to citizens via MyKad verification. Non-citizens and ineligible parties would be required to purchase diesel at unsubsidised prices.

items.title: King receives heartfelt Father's Day surprise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, received a special Father's Day gift from Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, as well as their children and grandchildren. "The meaningful gift reflected the love, appreciation and respect of his children and grandchildren towards His Majesty, who remains the pillar of the family. "The special moment also highlighted the close-knit family bond that continues to form the foundation of happiness and harmony within the royal household," read a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page.

items.title: Khaled: Malbatt shielded from ground conflict in Lebanon
items.summary: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the battalion's positioning reduces the risk of Malaysian forces becoming involved in any ground fighting in the event of unrest along the Lebanon-Israel border. He said areas closer to the border are instead monitored by peacekeeping forces from other countries, including France and Indonesia. Those deployed closer to the border include French and Indonesian forces.

items.title: Dr Sim urges deaf community to harness emerging technologies as Sarawak works to improve accessibility for PwDs
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — Sarawak still has significant ground to cover in improving infrastructure and accessibility for persons with disabilities (PwDs), particularly members of the deaf community, said Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian. “The deaf community is an integral part of Anak Sarawak. Dr Sim also expressed hope that the deaf community would be able to fully harness emerging technologies, particularly in the era of artificial intelligence.

items.title: Three Malaysians busted hours after entering Singapore on scam 'mission'
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Three Malaysian men were arrested within six hours of entering Singapore after allegedly arriving on instructions from a scam syndicate to collect cash and gold bars from victims and withdraw illicit proceeds from automated teller machines (ATMs). The suspects, aged 22 to 24, were charged yesterday over their alleged involvement in cross-border scam operations. Investigations led officers to two alleged accomplices who had entered Singapore with him.

items.title: PN to discuss Bersatu's position at key leadership meeting tomorrow
items.summary: TEMERLOH: The Perikatan Nasional (PN) leadership council will discuss Bersatu's position in the coalition at a meeting tomorrow, says Pas vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar. Samsuri, who is also PN chairman, said Bersatu would ultimately decide whether it wished to remain in or leave the opposition coalition. Samsuri said PN's composition remained unchanged, comprising Pas, Bersatu, the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) and Gerakan.

items.title: Perikatan to review coalition membership, logo and Johor, Negeri Sembilan poll strategy at emergency meeting tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) will convene an emergency meeting tomorrow to determine the coalition’s next steps, including matters related to its logo for the upcoming state elections and party membership. According to a meeting notice dated Thursday and reported by The Star , PN secretary‑general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the supreme council will meet at 8.30pm tomorrow at the PAS headquarters in Kuala Lumpur. PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa confirmed the meeting when contacted, while Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP) president P. Punithan said the discussion would cover the “Johor and Negeri Sembilan polls, the coalition logo and the way forward.” PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar was quoted in news reports as saying that the agenda includes membership matters involving Bersatu, adding that “as of now, the composition of Perikatan remains as it is.” The emergency meeting comes amid ongoing tensions between PAS and Bersatu over the coalition’s leadership.

items.title: Pakistani premier arrives in Switzerland for US-Iran talks
items.summary: KARACHI: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif arrived in Switzerland on Sunday morning to attend technical-level talks between the United States and Iran to end the armed conflict in the Middle East, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing local media. Sharif, along with army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir, will represent Pakistan in the talks on the implementation of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding, at Burgenstock, a statement from Pakistan's Foreign Ministry said. The US and Iran had been set to hold the talks in Switzerland on Friday to start a 60-day negotiation period, before the talks were postponed.

items.title: KOPMEDIA sets RM10mil revenue goal, eyes top 50 ranking
items.summary: Chairman Mohd Fadzil Abdul Ghani said the target is achievable through the cooperative's diversified business portfolio and strong support from Media Prima Bhd employees. He said KOPMEDIA is also venturing into new fields, including human capital development and mobile journalist courses, while continuing to prioritise the welfare of its members. Commenting on the cooperative's performance, Farhan said KOPMEDIA has shown consistent growth for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2025, by exploring new opportunities and providing value-added programmes for its members.

items.title: Former Titi Serong assemblyman Hasnul Zulkarnain rejoins Amanah after six years
items.summary: IPOH, June 21 — Former Titi Serong state assemblyman Hasnul Zulkarnain Abd Munaim has rejoined Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) after leaving the party for nearly six years. Perak Amanah chairman Datuk Asmuni Awi said the decision to readmit Hasnul Zulkarnain, who is also the former state Amanah Youth chief, was agreed upon during the party’s National Management Meeting and National Leadership Meeting on June 18. Asmuni said Hasnul Zulkarnain’s re-entry is expected to strengthen the party, as many former members who had left Amanah still share the same loyalty and belief in the party’s struggle.

items.title: The 15-day deficit: How Sabah’s shorter birth registration window is fuelling a statelessness crisis
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 30 — Advocates for Non-Discrimination and Access to Knowledge (Anak) Executive Director Anne Baltazar said unequal and complicated birth registration procedures, together with weak laws, are actively creating and deepening a crisis affecting thousands of children across Sabah. While parents in Peninsular Malaysia have 60 days to register a birth, Sabah allows only 45 days. Anak executive director Anne Baltazar highlights the 45-day period for parents to register births in the state with poor road access compared to 60 days in peninsular Malaysia.

items.title: Speaker confirms Hamzah’s status as Opposition Leader, Muhyiddin moved to new seat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul confirmed that he had received a notification letter regarding Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin’s status as the Opposition Leader. Speaking to Bernama, he said the Dewan Rakyat sitting, due to start tomorrow, will involve several changes to MPs’ seating positions. PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said during the recent Reset Malaysia Convention that Hamzah had resumed his duties as Opposition Leader.

items.title: From abah to abi: Malaysia’s top leaders salute the silent superheroes who don’t wear capes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — Deputy Prime Ministers Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, together with Cabinet ministers, today paid tribute to fathers in conjunction with Father’s Day, recognising their contributions and sacrifices. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said behind a father’s calm demeanour lie countless sacrifices made to ensure the well-being of the family, nurture children and safeguard their future. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil also extended Father’s Day greetings to all fathers.

items.title: Ganja buds: Terengganu police seize luxury rides and RM1.76m in drugs from Kampung Duyong Besar brothers
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 21 — Police have arrested two brothers suspected of using their house in Kampung Duyong Besar here as a storage facility for drugs worth RM1.76 million. Terengganu police chief Datuk Mohd Khairi Khairudin said the two, aged 39 and 50, were arrested together with about 22 kilogrammes of suspected ganja buds during a raid at about 3.30 pm on June 14 under Ops Tapis Khas. He said both suspects tested negative for drugs.

items.title: Diabetes remains one of Malaysia’s biggest public health challenges, says Sarawak Health director
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — Non-communicable diseases (NCDs), particularly diabetes, continue to pose major public health challenges in Malaysia. Sarawak State Health Department director Dr Veronica Lugah said data collected nationwide showed a significant number of Malaysians are affected by NCDs. One in six Malaysians has diabetes.

items.title: Army freezes hand‑grenade drills as investigation into Kedah training blast is fast‑tracked, says defence minister
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 21 — The Malaysian Army has temporarily suspended all hand grenade training activities following a fatal blast during a training session at Hobart Camp, Kedah, which claimed the lives of two personnel. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the order is effective immediately and will remain in place until the board of inquiry completes a comprehensive investigation into the cause of the incident. “As a result of the incident, the Army has issued an order to suspend all training involving hand grenades until the investigation is completed.

items.title: Kuching South mayor urges public to report murky water issues to Sarawak Water, not on social media
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — Members of the public are urged to lodge formal complaints with Sarawak Water Sdn Bhd whenever they encounter water supply issues such as murky water. According to Wee, Sarawak Water has been publishing notices and updates to help the public better understand water supply management. The mayor said Sarawak Water has been working with various contractors to address water supply issues and identify appropriate solutions.

items.title: Scratch-and-lose: Bau woman poorer by RM6,000 after falling for online ‘Super Lotto’ scam
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — A woman from a village in Bau lost over RM6,000 to an online scratch-and-win scam advertised under the name ‘Super Lotto’. Bau police chief Supt Mohd Haide A. Rahman said the victim, 35, came across an advertisement on Facebook on June 14 promoting the ‘Super Lotto’ competition. He said to join the competition, the victim was required to purchase 20 scratch-and-win tickets.

items.title: Kuching cop injured after falling into 6m‑deep gorge during village community programme
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — A police personnel suffered injuries to his body and head after he slipped and fell down a six-metre-deep gorge during a community programme in Kampung Sapit here yesterday. The Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) in a statement said it dispatched rescuers from the Siburan station following an emergency call at 12.20pm. “The victim was found to sustain injuries to his back, hands and head, and was carried out from the gorge on a stretcher,” said the department.

items.title: Malaysian sisters behind handcrafted footwear brand Machino named to Forbes 30 Under 30 Asia list
items.summary: Esther Tai Shen Hui and her younger sibling Amy Tai Shuh Yuk, who are cofounders of Malaysian footwear brand Machino, have solved a problem faced by women everywhere by making shoes that do away with the painful discomfort when wearing stylish shoes. “Getting into Forbes 30 Under 30 Asia 2026 is a proud moment for us,” said Esther, stressing that the recognition belongs to the whole team. Machino is picked by Forbes for its achievement in the retail and e-commerce category.

items.title: Pakatan to unveil Johor state poll candidates in Bukit Gambir tomorrow, says Anwar
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 21 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will attend the announcement ceremony for PH candidates for the 16th Johor State Election in Bukit Gambir, Tangkak, tomorrow. In a social media post today, Anwar said he would join other PH leaders at the event, scheduled for 8 pm at the Bukit Gambir Extreme Park field. “Insya-Allah, I will be in Bukit Gambir tomorrow to join Pakatan Harapan leaders in announcing the candidates who will carry the people’s mandate in the 16th Johor State Election.

items.title: Brics Malaysia: Russia’s supply assurance, Petronas-Turkmenistan gains from Anwar’s visits boost nation’s energy security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — Brics International Malaysia welcomes the positive outcomes of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s official visits to Russia and Turkmenistan, particularly in advancing Malaysia’s long-term energy security, enhancing oil and gas cooperation and strengthening strategic economic partnerships. Its chairman Jaziri Alkaf Abdillah Sufian said Anwar’s engagements with Russian President Vladimir Putin and Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov underscore Malaysia’s ability to maintain constructive relationships across major global blocs while advancing its sovereign economic priorities. Petronas’ continued presence in Turkmenistan reflects Malaysia’s established expertise in upstream energy operations, technology transfer, talent development and responsible resource management.

items.title: Anwar’s charisma emerges as diplomatic asset during Russia and Turkmenistan visits
items.summary: ISTANBUL, June 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s recent visit to Russia was marked not only by summit declarations, bilateral meetings and discussions on trade and energy but also by the notable reception he received on the international stage. Despite a packed schedule, Anwar stopped, listened and patiently answered. Anwar also took time to speak on his appreciation of Russian literature and poetry.

items.title: Bersama to announce Johor candidates Friday as applications pour in for two state polls, says Rafizi
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 21 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) will announce its line-up of candidates for the Johor state election in Johor Bahru on Friday. He said the party had received an encouraging response after opening applications slightly more than a week ago to individuals interested in contesting in the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections. The Election Commission has fixed July 11 as polling day for the Johor state election, with nomination day set for June 27 and early voting on July 7.

items.title: Pekan crash driver has six traffic summonses, including two for double‑line overtaking, say police
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 21 — The driver of a pick-up truck involved in yesterday’s fatal three-vehicle crash near Pekan has six traffic summonses to his name, including two for overtaking on a double line. Pekan police chief Supt Mohd Zaidi Mat Zain said the 31-year-old driver is still receiving treatment at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital here. Yesterday’s crash at KM67 Jalan Kuantan-Segamat, near Pekan, claimed three lives and left six others injured.
